content.claim: Special-education teacher training admission is high (2,944 first-choice applicants in 2024, ranking 8th nationally), but completion and workforce-entry rates are reported by union sources to be a small fraction (approximately one-fifth enter the profession, and fewer specialize in autism or state-sector roles)
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Union representative's claim about completion/placement rates (one-fifth) is stated without published administrative statistics
Pipeline lag between admission, program completion, and workforce entry (8 semesters) means 2024 admissions do not directly measure current workforce supply
No comparison provided with supply/demand ratios in other specialist professions
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
